briefs.title: Port Moody Council to recite Territorial Land Acknowledgement at meetings
briefs.summary: Port Moody City Council is set to open its meetings with a Territorial Land Acknowledgement — a formal statement recognizing that the city sits on the traditional and unceded (never formally surrendered) homelands of eight Indigenous Peoples, including the Kwikwetlem, Tsleil-Waututh, Musqueam, and Squamish Nations. This is a ceremonial agenda item and does not create new rules or obligations for local businesses.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Port Moody Agenda Adoption — April 14, 2026
briefs.summary: Port Moody City Council is set to vote on formally adopting the agenda for its April 14, 2026 meeting. This is a routine procedural step that sets the list of topics Council will discuss and decide on during that session. No specific business impact has been identified from this item alone.

briefs.title: Council to consider verbal update on Port Moody Arts, Culture, and Heritage Committee
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council is set to receive a verbal report from Councillor Kyla Knowles, Chair of the Arts, Culture, and Heritage Committee, at the April 14, 2026 meeting. This is an informational update — no bylaw or business regulation is currently proposed as part of this agenda item.

briefs.title: Council to Consider Extending City Support for Inlet Theatre Music Series Two More Years
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council will consider a request from the Inlet Music Series Team to extend existing city support for another two years (2026–2028). The proposed support includes waiving the venue rental fee (about $400 per concert) and continuing to promote the series in the city newsletter and calendar — the same arrangement that has been in place for the past four seasons.
briefs.action_required: If you support or oppose this city support extension, contact Port Moody Council before the April 14 meeting.

key_dates.label: Port Moody Council meeting — Inlet Music Series support extension delegation

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Declaring April 28 Official Day of Mourning for Workers
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council will consider a request from the New Westminster & District Labour Council to formally declare April 28 as the official 'Day of Mourning for Workers Killed and Injured on the Job.' The request also asks Council to observe a minute of silence at their April meeting and encourage staff to do the same at 11:00 a.m. on April 28.
briefs.action_required: If you employ workers, mark April 28 and consider observing a minute of silence at 11:00 a.m.

key_dates.label: Port Moody Council delegation presentation — Day of Mourning request
National Day of Mourning for Workers — minute of silence at 11:00 a.m.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Port Moody Public Input Rules for April 14, 2026 Meeting
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council will hold a public input period at its April 14, 2026 meeting where residents and business owners can speak for up to two minutes or submit written comments. Participants are expected to follow the City's respectful conduct policies, keeping feedback focused on issues rather than individuals.
briefs.action_required: Attend the April 14 Port Moody Council meeting to share your views in person or submit written input.

key_dates.label: Port Moody Council public input period — meeting date

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Approving Minutes from Four Closed Port Moody Council Meetings
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council is set to vote on formally approving the minutes from four recent meetings, including three closed-door sessions (meaning the public was not allowed in) held in February and March 2026, plus one regular public meeting from March 10, 2026. The closed sessions covered topics such as staff matters, land deals, legal advice, and negotiations with other governments — none of the details from those private discussions are made public.

briefs.title: Council to Consider Port Moody Consent Agenda Items — April 14, 2026
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council is set to vote on a group of items bundled together on the Consent Agenda at its April 14, 2026 meeting. Council can approve all items in a single vote, or any councillor can pull a specific item out for separate debate before the vote takes place.

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Port Moody Records Management Bylaw adoption
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council is set to vote on formally adopting Bylaw No. 3526, which would officially authorize how the City manages its records. The bylaw already passed its required readings in March 2026, and a new administrative policy approved by the City Manager would set the day-to-day rules for keeping city records in order.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision — this is an internal city bylaw with no direct business compliance requirement.

key_dates.label: Port Moody Records Management Bylaw No. 3526 — council adoption vote

briefs.title: Council to Consider Removing Old Parking Covenant from 112 James Road Development Site
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council is set to vote on removing a Section 219 Covenant — a legal restriction registered on the title of 112 James Road — that was originally tied to a place of worship (Jamaat Khana) that once operated on the site. Since the place of worship has been demolished and is relocating to a new site, and the property is already governed by updated zoning and development permit rules, staff say the old covenant is no longer needed and are asking Council to formally discharge (cancel) it.
briefs.action_required: If your business is near 112 James Road, watch for the Council decision — it clears the way for the mixed-use development to proceed.

briefs.title: Council to vote on renewing Port Moody's UBCM membership for 2026
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council is set to vote on renewing the city's membership in the Union of BC Municipalities (UBCM) — the organization that speaks up for local governments across BC. The proposed renewal would cost $16,269.38 for 2026, up slightly from $15,999.33 paid in 2025, and is already included in the city's budget.

briefs.title: Council to vote on 26-storey rental tower rezoning in Port Moody's Moody Centre
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council is set to vote on a rezoning bylaw that would allow Anthem Properties to build a large mixed-use rental development at 2914–2934 St. Johns Street, 85–87 Williams Street, and 2911–2925 Spring Street. The proposed project includes a 26-storey rental tower, a six-storey mixed-use building, and ground-floor retail and office space — totalling 321 rental units and about 31,861 sq ft of commercial space.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision on April 14 — it could affect nearby commercial leases and retail opportunities.

key_dates.label: Port Moody Council vote — Anthem Moody Centre rezoning bylaw first, second, and third readings

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Port Moody Arts and Business Initiative Progress Update
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council is set to receive an update on the Arts and Business Initiative, which aims to strengthen the city's 'City of the Arts' brand by connecting local artists and businesses. A specialist hired in August 2025 has been running pilot projects — including community events and a studio needs survey — and is on contract until October 2026, when a final report will be presented to Council.
briefs.action_required: If you are a Port Moody business or artist, watch for upcoming pilot projects and community events to get involved.

key_dates.label: Arts and Business Specialist contract end — final report due to Port Moody Council

Under city policy, non-health-related lighting requests like this one must receive Council approval before they can go ahead.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Port Moody Parks Committee Reviews 2026 Work Plan and Wharfs Bylaw
briefs.summary: Port Moody's Parks and Environment Committee met in January 2026 to review its draft work plan for the year. Topics on the table include a proposed Wharfs Bylaw (a rule governing the city's docks and waterfront structures) and the redevelopment of Kyle Center. No final decisions were made at this meeting — these items are still being discussed and will require further review.
briefs.action_required: Monitor Port Moody council updates if your business operates near waterfront or park areas.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Natural Resources, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Port Moody Council to Add Two Awareness Day Lighting Dates to City Schedule
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council has approved adding two special lighting dates to the city's schedule: green lights on May 16, 2026 for International Celiac Disease Awareness Day, and pink lights on September 13, 2026 for World Sepsis Day. These are ceremonial lighting requests and do not impose any requirements on local businesses.

key_dates.label: Port Moody — International Celiac Disease Awareness Day green lighting
Port Moody — World Sepsis Day pink lighting

briefs.title: Proposed: Metro Vancouver to Make Alcohol in Regional Parks Permanent
briefs.summary: Metro Vancouver's Board has directed staff to bring forward a bylaw — a local law — that would permanently allow adults to drink alcohol in clearly marked areas of five regional parks. This follows two successful pilot (trial) seasons in 2024 and 2025. The bylaw still needs to be drafted and approved before the change takes effect.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the upcoming bylaw vote — it could affect events or gatherings you plan at regional parks.

briefs.title: Proposed: Port Moody DCC and ACC Rate Hikes Could Halt Housing Projects
briefs.summary: Port Moody Council is set to consider major increases to Development Cost Charges (DCCs — fees developers pay to fund city infrastructure) and a new Amenity Cost Charges (ACC) program. Developers warn the proposed rates, especially the staff-recommended Option 5, could add millions in costs per project and make new housing developments financially unviable.
briefs.action_required: If you develop or build in Port Moody, attend the council meeting and submit written feedback before June 30, 2026.

key_dates.label: Port Moody DCC and ACC policy adoption target deadline
